perturbation.original_text: Company acknowledges and agrees that the reme dy at law for any breach of this NDA is inadequate and that, in additi on to monetary damages, including but not limited to special, incidental, consequential or puniti ve damages, and any other avai lable relief at law, whether based in contract, tort or otherwise, the State Bar will be entitled to specific performance, injunctive relief or any other eq uitable remedy without the need to (i) prove actual damages and/or (ii) post any bond or other security deposit in connection with such remedy.
perturbation.changed_text: Company acknowledges and agrees that the remedy at law for any breach of this NDA is inadequate and that the State Bar will be entitled to monetary damages, and any other available relief at law, whether based in contract, tort or otherwise.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the clause allowing for 'specific performance, injunctive relief or any other equitable remedy' limits the State Bar's legal recourse, contradicting standard legal practices that allow for equitable remedies in cases of breach of contract, particularly in situations involving confidential information. The waiver of the need to 'prove actual damages' and 'post any bond' is also omitted.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Civil Code § 3422 allows for injunctive relief to prevent the breach of an obligation. By removing the clause explicitly allowing for injunctive relief, the modified text contradicts this statute and limits the remedies available to the State Bar, particularly relevant in cases involving confidential information where monetary damages might be insufficient.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CIV 3422. skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Civil Code - CIV DIVISION 4. GENERAL PROVISIONS [3274 - 9566] ( Heading of Division 4 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 16. ) PART 1. RELIEF [3274 - 3428] ( Part 1 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 3. SPECIFIC AND PREVENTIVE RELIEF [3366 - 3424] ( Title 3 enacted 1872. ) CHAPTER 3. Preventive Relief [3420 - 3424] ( Chapter 3 enacted 1872. ) 3422. Except where otherwise provided by this Title, a final injunction may be granted to prevent the breach of an obligation existing in favor of the applicant: 1. Where pecuniary compensation would not afford adequate relief; 2. Where it would be extremely difficult to ascertain the amount of compensation which would afford adequate relief; 3. Where the restraint is necessary to prevent a multiplicity of judicial proceedings; or, 4. Where the obligation arises from a trust. (Enacted 1872.)
perturbation.justification: The modified text omits the clause that allows the State Bar to seek 'specific performance, injunctive relief or any other equitable remedy' for a breach of the NDA. California Civil Code § 3422, as stated in Scraped Law Snippet 1, allows for injunctive relief to prevent the breach of an obligation, particularly where monetary compensation is inadequate. By removing the possibility of injunctive relief, the modified contract contradicts this statute, as the omission directly limits the remedies available to the State Bar. This directly undermines the law's provision for injunctive relief in situations where monetary damages are insufficient, especially concerning confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: This NDA will be governed and constr ued in all respects in accordan ce with the laws of the State of California without regard to its conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.changed_text: This NDA will be governed and construed in all respects in accordance with principles of equity and fairness.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the explicit reference to California law and replacing it with 'principles of equity and fairness,' the contract creates ambiguity and potentially undermines the enforceability of the agreement under specific California statutes. This could jeopardize the State Bar's ability to rely on established legal precedents and protections afforded by California law.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Commercial Code § 1103 states that unless displaced by the particular provisions of the Commercial Code, the principles of law and equity supplement its provisions. Removing the explicit reference to California law and replacing it with 'principles of equity and fairness' attempts to bypass established California contract law and replace them with vague equitable principles, potentially contradicting established commercial code.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, COM 1103 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Commercial Code - COM DIVISION 1. GENERAL PROVISIONS [1101 - 1310] ( Division 1 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) CHAPTER 1. Short Title, Construction, Application and Subject Matter of the Code [1101 - 1108] ( Chapter 1 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) 1103. (a) This code shall be liberally construed and applied to promote its underlying purposes and policies, which are: (1) to simplify, clarify, and modernize the law governing commercial transactions; (2) to permit the continued expansion of commercial practices through custom, usage, and agreement of the parties; and (3) to make uniform the law among the various jurisdictions. (b) Unless displaced by the particular provisions of this code, the principles of law and equity, including the law merchant and the law relative to capacity to contract, principal and agent, estoppel, fraud, misrepresentation, duress, coercion, mistake, bankruptcy, and other validating or invalidating cause supplement its provisions. (Amended by Stats. 2006, Ch. 254, Sec. 9. Effective January 1, 2007.)
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that California law governs the NDA, providing a clear legal framework. The changed text replaces this with 'principles of equity and fairness,' removing the explicit tie to California law. This contradicts California Commercial Code § 1103, which indicates that the principles of law and equity supplement the Commercial Code, but do not replace it. The original text provides a solid legal base; the new text removes this, introducing ambiguity. The changed text weakens the enforceability of the agreement under California statutes, which is a significant legal risk.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, this NDA will no t prohibit Company from disclosing any Information if required by an appropriate legal or regulatory author ity, provided that, prior to any disclosure: (iii) Company provides the State Bar with prompt written notice of the required disclosure so that the State Bar may seek such protective order as it may deem appropriate; and (iv) disclosure of any Information is limited only to the extent legally required and Company uses its best efforts to obtain assurances that confidential treatment will be accorded such disclosed Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, this NDA will not prohibit Company from disclosing any Information if required by legal or regulatory authority.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the requirements for 'prompt written notice' to the State Bar and limiting disclosure 'only to the extent legally required' with 'best efforts to obtain assurances that confidential treatment will be accorded,' the modified text removes critical protections for the State Bar's confidential information. This weakens the State Bar's ability to prevent or mitigate unauthorized disclosures, increasing the risk of confidential data exposure.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Code of Civil Procedure § 2016.050 addresses protective orders. By removing the requirement for the company to notify the State Bar of legally required disclosures, the State Bar is unable to seek a protective order to limit the scope of the disclosure or ensure the confidentiality of the disclosed information is maintained to the fullest extent possible under the law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CCP 2016.050 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Code of Civil Procedure - CCP PART 4. MISCELLANEOUS PROVISIONS [1855 - 2107] ( Heading of Part 4 amended by Stats. 1965, Ch. 299. ) TITLE 4. CIVIL DISCOVERY ACT [2016.010 - 2036.050] ( Title 4 added by Stats. 2004, Ch. 182, Sec. 23. ) CHAPTER 1. General Provisions [2016.010 - 2016.090] ( Chapter 1 added by Stats. 2004, Ch. 182, Sec. 23. ) 2016.050. Sections 1011 and 1013 apply to any method of discovery or service of a motion provided for in this title. (Amended by Stats. 2017, Ch. 64, Sec. 2. (SB 543) Effective January 1, 2018.)
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the requirement for the Company to provide prompt written notice to the State Bar before disclosing confidential information, which eliminates the State Bar's ability to seek a protective order. The Law Explanation states that California Code of Civil Procedure § 2016.050 addresses protective orders, and by removing the notification requirement, the State Bar is unable to seek such an order. Scraped Law Snippet 1 only indicates the existence of CCP 2016.050 but does not provide the full content of the law, so we must rely upon the Law Explanation. The omission of the notification and limitation requirements creates a legally significant contradiction by removing the State Bar's ability to protect its confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: Company agrees that it will reta in all Information provided in connection with the RFP in the strictest confidence, and will neither use nor di sclose such Information to anyone other than those who, in each case, need to know the Information for the purpose of responding to this RFP. Company will use its best efforts to ensure th at such individuals adhere to the NDA, and Company agrees that it will be responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its employees, agents, authorized representatives or permitted assignees or subcontractors.
perturbation.changed_text: Company agrees that it will retain all Information provided in connection with the RFP in confidence, and will neither use nor disclose such Information to anyone. Company will use reasonable efforts to ensure that such individuals adhere to the NDA.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'strictest' from 'strictest confidence' and 'other than those who, in each case, need to know the Information for the purpose of responding to this RFP' weakens the protection of the confidential information and potentially allows for broader use/disclosure. Further, removing 'and Company agrees that it will be responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its employees, agents, authorized representatives or permitted assignees or subcontractors' limits liability for breaches of the NDA.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Civil Code § 1644 states that the words of a contract are to be understood in their ordinary and popular sense, rather than according to their strict legal meaning; unless used by the parties in a technical sense, or unless a special meaning is given to them by usage. By diminishing the strength of the confidentiality clause, the contract potentially weakens the scope of confidentiality obligations that a reasonable person would expect under such circumstances.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CIV 1644. skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Civil Code - CIV DIVISION 3. OBLIGATIONS [1427 - 3273.69] ( Heading of Division 3 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 14. ) PART 2. CONTRACTS [1549 - 1701] ( Part 2 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 3. INTERPRETATION OF CONTRACTS [1635 - 1663] ( Title 3 enacted 1872. ) 1644. The words of a contract are to be understood in their ordinary and popular sense, rather than according to their strict legal meaning; unless used by the parties in a technical sense, or unless a special meaning is given to them by usage, in which case the latter must be followed. (Enacted 1872.)
perturbation.justification: The modification significantly weakens the confidentiality obligations, creating a legal contradiction under the Omission Legal Contradiction definition. The original text commits the company to "strictest confidence", whereas the changed text omits "strictest," reducing the standard of care. Further, the original text limits disclosure to those with a need to know, whereas the changed text removes this limitation, potentially allowing for broader disclosure. The removal of the liability clause for breaches by employees, agents, etc., further weakens the protection. California Civil Code § 1644, as indicated in Scraped Law Snippet 1, states that contract words are to be understood in their ordinary and popular sense. A reasonable person would understand 'strictest confidence' to provide a higher level of protection than simply 'confidence,' and the removal of this higher standard, along with the limitation on disclosure, contradicts what a reasonable person would expect. Therefore, the modified text removes essential information related to the standard of confidentiality, potentially creating a legal loophole and undermining consumer protection by reducing the scope of confidentiality.
